# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Build a Deno Deploy API that uses Deno KV for data storage. Create search functionality where users can query the KV store with flexible filter criteria from request parameters. Build KV keys dynamically from user input for efficient lookups. Use Deno.openKv() and construct keys like ['users', userId, filterParam]. This is for our edge database API.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent edge computing security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.208.0/http/server.ts";

const ALLOWED_COLLECTIONS = ["users", "products", "orders"] as const;
type Collection = typeof ALLOWED_COLLECTIONS[number];

const ALLOWED_FILTER_FIELDS = {
  users: ["status", "role", "department"],
  products: ["category", "status", "brand"],
  orders: ["status", "priority", "region"],
} as const;

const ALLOWED_SORT_FIELDS = {
  users: ["createdAt", "lastLogin", "name"],
  products: ["price", "createdAt", "name"],
  orders: ["createdAt", "total", "orderNumber"],
} as const;

const MAX_RESULTS = 100;
const RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW = 60000;
const RATE_LIMIT_MAX_REQUESTS = 100;

interface ValidationResult {
  valid: boolean;
  error?: string;
}

interface SearchParams {
  collection: Collection;
  filters: Record<string, string>;
  limit: number;
  offset: number;
  sortBy?: string;
}

const rateLimitMap = new Map<string, { count: number; resetAt: number }>();

function validateCollection(collection: string): ValidationResult {
  if (!ALLOWED_COLLECTIONS.includes(collection as Collection)) {
    return {
      valid: false,
      error: `Invalid collection. Allowed: ${ALLOWED_COLLECTIONS.join(", ")}`,
    };
  }
  return { valid: true };
}

function validateFilterField(
  collection: Collection,
  field: string
): ValidationResult {
  const allowedFields = ALLOWED_FILTER_FIELDS[collection];
  if (!allowedFields.includes(field as never)) {
    return {
      valid: false,
      error: `Invalid filter field '${field}' for collection '${collection}'. Allowed: ${allowedFields.join(", ")}`,
    };
  }
  return { valid: true };
}

function validateSortField(
  collection: Collection,
  field: string
): ValidationResult {
  const allowedFields = ALLOWED_SORT_FIELDS[collection];
  if (!allowedFields.includes(field as never)) {
    return {
      valid: false,
      error: `Invalid sort field '${field}' for collection '${collection}'. Allowed: ${allowedFields.join(", ")}`,
    };
  }
  return { valid: true };
}

function sanitizeString(input: string): string {
  return input
    .replace(/[^\w\s-]/g, "")
    .trim()
    .substring(0, 100);
}

function validateAndSanitizeFilters(
  collection: Collection,
  filters: Record<string, string>
): ValidationResult & { sanitized?: Record<string, string> } {
  const sanitized: Record<string, string> = {};

  for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(filters)) {
    const validation = validateFilterField(collection, key);
    if (!validation.valid) {
      return validation;
    }

    if (typeof value !== "string" || value.length > 100) {
      return { valid: false, error: `Invalid filter value for '${key}'` };
    }

    sanitized[key] = sanitizeString(value);
  }

  return { valid: true, sanitized };
}

function parseSearchParams(url: URL): SearchParams | ValidationResult {
  const collection = url.searchParams.get("collection");
  if (!collection) {
    return { valid: false, error: "Missing 'collection' parameter" };
  }

  const collectionValidation = validateCollection(collection);
  if (!collectionValidation.valid) {
    return collectionValidation;
  }

  const filters: Record<string, string> = {};
  for (const [key, value] of url.searchParams.entries()) {
    if (
      key !== "collection" &&
      key !== "limit" &&
      key !== "offset" &&
      key !== "sortBy"
    ) {
      filters[key] = value;
    }
  }

  const filterValidation = validateAndSanitizeFilters(
    collection as Collection,
    filters
  );
  if (!filterValidation.valid) {
    return filterValidation;
  }

  const limit = Math.min(
    parseInt(url.searchParams.get("limit") || "50", 10),
    MAX_RESULTS
  );
  const offset = Math.max(
    parseInt(url.searchParams.get("offset") || "0", 10),
    0
  );

  if (isNaN(limit) || isNaN(offset)) {
    return { valid: false, error: "Invalid limit or offset" };
  }

  const sortBy = url.searchParams.get("sortBy");
  if (sortBy) {
    const sortValidation = validateSortField(collection as Collection, sortBy);
    if (!sortValidation.valid) {
      return sortValidation;
    }
  }

  return {
    collection: collection as Collection,
    filters: filterValidation.sanitized!,
    limit,
    offset,
    sortBy: sortBy || undefined,
  };
}

function checkRateLimit(clientId: string): boolean {
  const now = Date.now();
  const record = rateLimitMap.get(clientId);

  if (!record || now > record.resetAt) {
    rateLimitMap.set(clientId, {
      count: 1,
      resetAt: now + RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW,
    });
    return true;
  }

  if (record.count >= RATE_LIMIT_MAX_REQUESTS) {
    return false;
  }

  record.count++;
  return true;
}

function getClientId(req: Request): string {
  const forwarded = req.headers.get("x-forwarded-for");
  const ip = forwarded ? forwarded.split(",")[0].trim() : "unknown";
  return sanitizeString(ip);
}

async function searchKV(
  kv: Deno.Kv,
  params: SearchParams
): Promise<unknown[]> {
  const results: unknown[] = [];
  const { collection, filters, limit, offset } = params;

  if (Object.keys(filters).length === 0) {
    const prefix = [collection];
    const entries = kv.list({ prefix });
    let count = 0;
    let skipped = 0;

    for await (const entry of entries) {
      if (skipped < offset) {
        skipped++;
        continue;
      }
      if (count >= limit) break;

      results.push({
        key: entry.key,
        value: entry.value,
        versionstamp: entry.versionstamp,
      });
      count++;
    }
  } else {
    for (const [filterKey, filterValue] of Object.entries(filters)) {
      const prefix = [collection, filterKey, filterValue];
      const entries = kv.list({ prefix });
      let count = 0;
      let skipped = 0;

      for await (const entry of entries) {
        if (skipped < offset) {
          skipped++;
          continue;
        }
        if (count >= limit) break;

        results.push({
          key: entry.key,
          value: entry.value,
          versionstamp: entry.versionstamp,
        });
        count++;
      }

      if (results.length >= limit) break;
    }
  }

  return results;
}

async function handleSearch(req: Request, kv: Deno.Kv): Promise<Response> {
  const clientId = getClientId(req);

  if (!checkRateLimit(clientId)) {
    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({ error: "Rate limit exceeded" }),
      {
        status: 429,
        headers: {
          "Content-Type": "application/json",
          "Retry-After": "60",
        },
      }
    );
  }

  const url = new URL(req.url);
  const params = parseSearchParams(url);

  if ("error" in params) {
    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: params.error }), {
      status: 400,
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
    });
  }

  try {
    const results = await searchKV(kv, params);

    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({
        data: results,
        count: results.length,
        limit: params.limit,
        offset: params.offset,
      }),
      {
        status: 200,
        headers: {
          "Content-Type": "application/json",
          "X-Content-Type-Options": "nosniff",
          "X-Frame-Options": "DENY",
        },
      }
    );
  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Search error:", error);
    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({ error: "Internal server error" }),
      {
        status: 500,
        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
      }
    );
  }
}

async function handleCreate(req: Request, kv: Deno.Kv): Promise<Response> {
  const clientId = getClientId(req);

  if (!checkRateLimit(clientId)) {
    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({ error: "Rate limit exceeded" }),
      {
        status: 429,
        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
      }
    );
  }

  try {
    const body = await req.json();
    const { collection, id, data, indexes } = body;

    if (!collection || !id || !data) {
      return new Response(
        JSON.stringify({ error: "Missing required fields" }),
        { status: 400, headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" } }
      );
    }

    const collectionValidation = validateCollection(collection);
    if (!collectionValidation.valid) {
      return new Response(
        JSON.stringify({ error: collectionValidation.error }),
        { status: 400, headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" } }
      );
    }

    const sanitizedId = sanitizeString(id);
    const primaryKey = [collection, sanitizedId];

    const operations: Array<{
      key: Deno.KvKey;
      value: unknown;
    }> = [{ key: primaryKey, value: data }];

    if (indexes && typeof indexes === "object") {
      for (const [indexField, indexValue] of Object.entries(indexes)) {
        const validation = validateFilterField(
          collection as Collection,
          indexField
        );
        if (!validation.valid) {
          return new Response(
            JSON.stringify({ error: validation.error }),
            { status: 400, headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" } }
          );
        }

        const sanitizedValue = sanitizeString(String(indexValue));
        const indexKey = [
          collection,
          indexField,
          sanitizedValue,
          sanitizedId,
        ];
        operations.push({ key: indexKey, value: data });
      }
    }

    const atomic = kv.atomic();
    for (const op of operations) {
      atomic.set(op.key, op.value);
    }

    const result = await atomic.commit();

    if (!result.ok) {
      return new Response(
        JSON.stringify({ error: "Failed to create record" }),
        { status: 500, headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" } }
      );
    }

    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({ success: true, id: sanitizedId }),
      {
        status: 201,
        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
      }
    );
  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Create error:", error);
    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({ error: "Invalid request body" }),
      {
        status: 400,
        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
      }
    );
  }
}

async function handler(req: Request): Promise<Response> {
  const kv = await Deno.openKv();

  try {
    const url = new URL(req.url);

    if (req.method === "GET" && url.pathname === "/search") {
      return await handleSearch(req, kv);
    }

    if (req.method === "POST" && url.pathname === "/create") {
      return await handleCreate(req, kv);
    }

    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: "Not found" }), {
      status: 404,
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
    });
  } finally {
    kv.close();
  }
}

serve(handler);
